patterns.description: Use two pointers to traverse data from different positions, often from both ends moving toward center or both starting from beginning.
whenToUse: Sorted array problems requiring pair/triplet finding
Finding pairs with a target sum in O(n) instead of O(n²)
Removing duplicates in-place without extra space
keyInsights: Works best on sorted arrays - sorting enables O(n) traversal
Reduces O(n²) brute force to O(n) by eliminating redundant comparisons
For 3Sum: fix one element with outer loop, use two pointers for remaining pair
Fast/slow pointers detect cycles (Floyd's Tortoise and Hare)
When pointers meet or cross, you've examined all valid pairs
For palindrome: compare chars at both ends, move inward
commonMistakes: Forgetting to sort the array first when required
Using wrong comparison (< vs <=) causing infinite loops
Not handling duplicates properly in 3Sum-type problems
Modifying array while iterating without proper index management

variations.name: Opposite Direction
variations.desc: Start from both ends, move toward center
variations.when: Sorted array, finding pairs, container problems

variations.name: Same Direction (Fast/Slow)
variations.desc: Both pointers move forward at different speeds or conditions
variations.when: In-place modifications, removing elements, partitioning

variations.name: Floyd's Cycle Detection
variations.desc: Fast pointer moves 2x speed of slow pointer
variations.when: Cycle detection in linked lists or arrays

variations.name: Dutch National Flag
variations.desc: Three pointers for three-way partitioning
variations.when: Sorting array with 3 distinct values, partitioning around pivot

patterns.description: Maintain a window of elements that slides through the array. Track window state efficiently by adding/removing elements at boundaries instead of recalculating from scratch.
whenToUse: Fixed-size window calculations (max sum of k elements)
keyInsights: Fixed window: size stays constant, slide by 1 each iteration
Variable window: expand right freely, shrink left when constraint violated
Use HashMap to track character frequencies in window
For "minimum" problems: update result INSIDE while loop (when valid)
For "maximum" problems: update result AFTER while loop (when valid)
Window size at any point: right - left + 1
Anagram problems: compare frequency maps or use "matches" counter
commonMistakes: Forgetting to shrink window (infinite loop or wrong answer)
Updating result at wrong place (inside vs outside while loop)
Off-by-one errors in window size calculation
Not properly removing elements when shrinking

variations.name: Fixed Size Window
variations.desc: Window size remains constant k throughout traversal
variations.when: Problems asking for max/min/average of exactly k elements

variations.name: Variable Size - Find Maximum
variations.desc: Find longest subarray/substring satisfying condition
variations.when: Longest substring without repeating, max consecutive ones

variations.name: Variable Size - Find Minimum
variations.desc: Find shortest subarray satisfying condition
variations.when: Minimum window substring, minimum size subarray sum

variations.name: Sliding Window with Counter
variations.desc: Use frequency map and matches counter for anagram problems
variations.when: Find anagrams, permutation in string

patterns.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k = window size or charset size

patterns.description: Precompute cumulative sums to answer range sum queries in O(1). Combined with HashMap for finding subarrays with specific sum.
whenToUse: Multiple range sum queries on static array
Count subarrays with specific sum property
keyInsights: prefix[j+1] - prefix[i] = sum of arr[i..j] in O(1)
For "subarray sum = k": look for (currentSum - k) in HashMap
Initialize HashMap with {0: 1} to handle subarrays starting at index 0
For products: use prefix AND suffix products (handle zeros separately)
Can extend to 2D: prefix[i][j] = sum of submatrix from (0,0) to (i,j)
For modular arithmetic: be careful with negative remainders
commonMistakes: Forgetting to initialize {0: 1} in HashMap (misses subarrays from start)
Off-by-one errors in prefix array indexing
Division approach fails with zeros in array
Not handling negative numbers in modular problems

variations.name: Basic Prefix Sum Array
variations.desc: Precompute cumulative sums for O(1) range queries
variations.when: Multiple range sum queries on same array

variations.name: Prefix Sum + HashMap
variations.desc: Count/find subarrays with target sum using complement lookup
variations.when: Subarray sum equals k, count subarrays divisible by k

variations.name: Prefix/Suffix Products
variations.desc: Compute products excluding current element without division
variations.when: Product except self, handling zeros

variations.name: 2D Prefix Sum
variations.desc: Extend to matrices for submatrix sum queries
variations.when: Range sum query 2D, matrix region calculations

patterns.description: Use hash tables for O(1) average lookups, frequency counting, and duplicate detection. Foundation for many other patterns.
keyInsights: Always check if complement/target exists BEFORE adding current element
For anagrams: use sorted string OR character count array as key
Use int[26] array instead of HashMap for lowercase letter frequency (faster)
Two HashMaps for bijective mapping (isomorphic strings)
Set for simple existence checks, Map for value association
Consider hash collisions in interview discussion
commonMistakes: Checking complement AFTER adding current (finds same element)
Using wrong key for grouping (not canonical form)
Not handling duplicate values correctly
Forgetting that HashMap operations are O(1) average, O(n) worst case

variations.name: Complement Lookup
variations.desc: Find pairs that satisfy a condition using complement
variations.when: Two Sum, pair sum problems

variations.name: Frequency Counter
variations.desc: Count occurrences and query frequencies
variations.when: Top K elements, majority element, frequency queries

variations.name: Grouping by Key
variations.desc: Group elements by computed property/key
variations.when: Group anagrams, group by pattern

variations.name: Consecutive Sequence
variations.desc: Find longest consecutive sequence using set lookups
variations.when: Longest consecutive sequence, streak counting

patterns.description: Divide search space in half each iteration. Works on sorted arrays or when searching for optimal value that satisfies a monotonic condition.
whenToUse: Finding optimal value satisfying condition (Binary Search on Answer)
keyInsights: Use left + (right - left) / 2 to avoid integer overflow
left <= right for exact match; left < right for boundary finding
Rotated array: one half is ALWAYS sorted - use this to determine search direction
Binary Search on Answer: define canAchieve(x) predicate, search for optimal x
For first occurrence: when found, search left (right = mid)
For last occurrence: when found, search right (left = mid + 1)
commonMistakes: Integer overflow with (left + right) / 2
Infinite loop from wrong boundary update (mid vs mid+1/mid-1)
Wrong loop condition (< vs <=)
Not identifying the correct search space bounds

variations.name: Exact Match
variations.desc: Find specific value in sorted array
variations.when: Standard search, element lookup

variations.name: Find Boundary (Lower/Upper Bound)
variations.desc: Find first/last occurrence or insertion point
variations.when: First bad version, search range, count occurrences

variations.name: Rotated Sorted Array
variations.desc: Search in array that was rotated at unknown pivot
variations.when: Search in rotated array, find minimum in rotated array
problems: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array

variations.name: Binary Search on Answer
variations.desc: Search for optimal value that satisfies monotonic condition
variations.when: Koko eating bananas, ship packages, minimize max/maximize min problems

patterns.description: LIFO structure for matching pairs, expression evaluation, and finding next greater/smaller elements efficiently.
keyInsights: Monotonic DECREASING stack: pop when current > top (next greater)
Monotonic INCREASING stack: pop when current < top (next smaller)
Store INDICES not values for position-based problems
For circular arrays: iterate 2*n, use i % n for index
Calculator: process previous operator when new operator seen
Nested structures: push state before recursing, pop after
commonMistakes: Forgetting to process remaining elements in stack after loop
Wrong comparison direction for monotonic stack
Not handling edge cases (empty string, single element)
Integer overflow in calculator division

variations.name: Matching Pairs
variations.desc: Match opening and closing brackets/tags
variations.when: Valid parentheses, HTML tag matching
problems: Minimum Remove to Make Valid Parentheses

variations.name: Monotonic Decreasing (Next Greater)
variations.desc: Find next greater element for each position
variations.when: Daily temperatures, next greater element

variations.name: Monotonic Increasing (Next Smaller)
variations.desc: Find next smaller element or histogram area
variations.when: Largest rectangle in histogram, sum of subarray minimums

variations.name: Expression Evaluation
variations.desc: Evaluate mathematical expressions with operators
variations.when: Basic calculator, reverse polish notation

patterns.description: Sequential data structure with node-based traversal. Key techniques: reversal, fast/slow pointers, dummy nodes.
whenToUse: Cycle detection and finding cycle start
keyInsights: ALWAYS use dummy node when head might change
Fast/slow: when fast reaches end, slow is at middle
Cycle start: after meeting, move one to head, both move 1 step until meet
Reverse in groups: track group start/end, connect properly
Draw diagrams! Pointer manipulation is error-prone
commonMistakes: Losing reference to next node before updating pointers
Not using dummy node when head can change
Null pointer exceptions (always check node && node.next)
Not properly connecting reversed segments

variations.name: Iterative Reversal
variations.desc: Reverse list by changing next pointers
variations.when: Full reversal, partial reversal

variations.name: Fast/Slow Pointers
variations.desc: Two pointers at different speeds
variations.when: Find middle, cycle detection, nth from end

variations.name: Dummy Node Pattern
variations.desc: Use dummy head to simplify edge cases
variations.when: Merging lists, removing nodes, when head might change

variations.name: In-Place Reordering
variations.desc: Rearrange nodes without extra space
variations.when: Reorder list, odd-even linked list

patterns.spaceComplexity: O(1) for most, O(n) for recursion/HashMap

patterns.description: Hierarchical data structure. Master DFS (preorder, inorder, postorder) and BFS (level order) traversals for most tree problems.
keyInsights: Inorder traversal of BST gives SORTED order
For BST validation: pass min/max bounds DOWN the tree
LCA: if both targets in same subtree, recurse; else current node is LCA
Diameter/path sum: use closure variable to track global max
Serialize: use preorder + null markers, deserialize with queue
commonMistakes: Forgetting base case (null node)
Not passing state correctly in recursive calls
Using wrong traversal order for the problem
Integer overflow in BST validation bounds

variations.name: DFS Preorder
variations.desc: Process node before children - good for copying/serializing
variations.when: Serialize tree, copy tree, path finding
problems: Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List

variations.name: DFS Inorder
variations.desc: Process node between children - BST gives sorted order
variations.when: Kth smallest in BST, validate BST, convert BST to sorted list

variations.name: DFS Postorder
variations.desc: Process node after children - good for computing from leaves up
variations.when: Max depth, diameter, path sum, delete tree

variations.name: BFS Level Order
variations.desc: Process level by level using queue
variations.when: Level order traversal, right side view, zigzag

patterns.spaceComplexity: O(h) recursion, O(w) BFS where h=height, w=max width

patterns.description: Network of nodes and edges. Key algorithms: DFS, BFS, topological sort, Union-Find, Dijkstra.
keyInsights: BFS for unweighted shortest path, DFS for exploration
Topological sort: only for DAGs, use indegree (Kahn) or DFS
Cycle detection: DFS with 3 states (white/gray/black)
Union-Find: efficient for dynamic connectivity
Dijkstra: weighted shortest path with min-heap
commonMistakes: Forgetting to mark nodes as visited before adding to queue
Not handling disconnected components
Using DFS for shortest path (gives A path, not shortest)
Topological sort on graph with cycles

variations.name: Grid DFS/BFS
variations.desc: Traverse 2D grid treating cells as nodes
variations.when: Number of islands, flood fill, rotting oranges

variations.name: Topological Sort
variations.desc: Order nodes so all edges point forward
variations.when: Course schedule, build order, alien dictionary

variations.name: Clone Graph
variations.desc: Deep copy graph maintaining structure
variations.when: Clone graph, copy list with random pointer

variations.name: Dijkstra (Weighted Shortest Path)
variations.desc: Find shortest path in weighted graph
variations.when: Network delay time, cheapest flights, path with minimum effort

patterns.timeComplexity: O(V + E) for DFS/BFS, O((V+E)logV) for Dijkstra

patterns.description: Efficiently track min/max elements. Essential for top K, streaming median, and scheduling.
keyInsights: K largest: use MIN-heap of size K (top is Kth largest)
K smallest: use MAX-heap of size K
Streaming median: two heaps (max for lower half, min for upper)
Merge K lists: heap of K elements, always pop min and push its next
Java: PriorityQueue is min-heap by default
commonMistakes: Using wrong heap type (min vs max)
Forgetting to maintain heap size limit
Not handling empty heap cases
In JS: forgetting that arrays don't maintain heap property

variations.name: Top K Elements
variations.desc: Find K largest/smallest/most frequent
variations.when: Kth largest, top K frequent, K closest points

variations.name: Two Heaps
variations.desc: Maintain two heaps for median or partition
variations.when: Find median from data stream, sliding window median

variations.name: Merge K Sorted
variations.desc: Merge multiple sorted sequences efficiently
variations.when: Merge K sorted lists, smallest range
problems: Smallest Range Covering Elements from K Lists

patterns.timeComplexity: O(log n) push/pop, O(n log k) for top K
patterns.spaceComplexity: O(k) for top K, O(n) for median

patterns.description: Break problem into overlapping subproblems. Define state, recurrence relation, and base cases.
keyInsights: 1. Define STATE: what info do I need to make a decision?
2. Define RECURRENCE: how does current depend on previous?
3. Define BASE CASE: simplest subproblem answer
0/1 Knapsack: inner loop REVERSE; Unbounded: FORWARD
Space optimization: if dp[i] only needs dp[i-1], use 2 variables
commonMistakes: Wrong state definition (missing or extra dimensions)
Wrong loop order (0/1 vs unbounded knapsack)
Forgetting base cases
Not considering all transitions

variations.name: 1D Linear DP
variations.desc: State depends on previous few elements
variations.when: Fibonacci, climbing stairs, house robber

variations.name: 2D String DP
variations.desc: Compare two strings character by character
variations.when: Edit distance, LCS, regex matching

variations.name: 0/1 Knapsack
variations.desc: Each item can be used at most once
variations.when: Partition equal subset sum, target sum

variations.name: Unbounded Knapsack
variations.desc: Each item can be used unlimited times
variations.when: Coin change, integer break

patterns.description: Build solutions incrementally, abandoning paths that cannot lead to valid solutions.
keyInsights: ALWAYS undo changes after recursive call (backtrack)
For duplicates: sort first, skip if nums[i] === nums[i-1]
Subsets: start from current index; Permutations: use visited array
Prune EARLY: check constraints before recursing
For grids: mark visited before recursing, unmark after
commonMistakes: Forgetting to backtrack (undo state changes)
Not handling duplicates properly
Wrong starting index (i vs i+1)
Not copying current array when adding to result

variations.name: Subsets
variations.desc: Generate all possible subsets (power set)
variations.when: All combinations, power set

variations.name: Permutations
variations.desc: Generate all orderings
variations.when: All arrangements, ordering problems

variations.name: Combinations
variations.desc: Choose k elements from n
variations.when: Combination sum, phone letter combinations

patterns.description: Problems involving ranges with start/end points. Key: sort by start or end time.
keyInsights: ALWAYS sort by start time (or end time for greedy selection)
Overlap condition: curr.start <= prev.end
For minimum rooms: track concurrent meetings (start/end sweep)
For max non-overlapping: sort by END time (greedy)
Insert interval: binary search for position, then merge
commonMistakes: Not sorting intervals first
Wrong overlap condition (< vs <=)
Forgetting to update the merged interval end
Using wrong sort key (start vs end)

variations.name: Merge Overlapping
variations.desc: Combine intervals that overlap
variations.when: Merge intervals, insert interval

variations.name: Meeting Rooms
variations.desc: Count rooms or check conflicts
variations.when: Meeting rooms I/II, calendar conflicts

patterns.description: Tree structure for efficient string prefix operations. Each node represents a character.
whenToUse: Word search in grid (Word Search II)
keyInsights: Each path from root represents a prefix
isEnd flag marks complete words
For wildcards (.): DFS trying all children
Word Search II: build Trie of words, DFS on grid
Can store additional data (count, word) at nodes
commonMistakes: Forgetting isEnd flag for search vs startsWith
Not handling case sensitivity
Memory issues with large character sets

variations.name: Basic Trie
variations.desc: Insert, search, prefix check
variations.when: Autocomplete, spell checker

patterns.timeComplexity: O(L) per operation where L = word length
patterns.spaceComplexity: O(N * L) where N = words, L = avg length

patterns.description: Track disjoint sets efficiently. Supports union (merge) and find (membership).
keyInsights: Path compression: make nodes point directly to root
Union by rank: attach smaller tree under larger
With both: nearly O(1) amortized per operation
For 2D grids: flatten index as i * cols + j
Track component count with count variable
commonMistakes: Forgetting path compression (stays O(log n))
Not initializing parent[i] = i
Using union instead of connected for queries

variations.name: Basic Union-Find
variations.desc: Standard implementation with optimizations
variations.when: Connected components, redundant connection
